changesProposed.name: DCVC (Data Collective)

verified.name: DCVC
verified.thesis: DCVC backs entrepreneurs using AI to unlock potential across sectors. The firm invests in companies solving trillion-dollar problems at the intersection of computational approaches and data. Investment areas include AI, climate, engineering, materials science, robotics, space, water, biology, and defense.
verified.notes: Source [1] states 'For 15 years, DCVC has backed brilliant entrepreneurs' which suggests founding around 2009-2010 if current date is 2024-2025, but no explicit founding year provided in sources. No flagship fund name explicitly stated in sources. HQ location inferred from website domain and standard VC firm location references but not explicitly stated in provided excerpts.

changes.current: DCVC backs entrepreneurs using AI to unlock potential across sectors. The firm invests in companies solving trillion-dollar problems at the intersection of computational approaches and massive datasets. Focus areas include AI, climate, engineering, materials science, robotics, space, water, biology, defense and security.
changes.verified: DCVC backs entrepreneurs using AI to unlock potential across sectors. The firm invests in companies solving trillion-dollar problems at the intersection of computational approaches and data. Investment areas include AI, climate, engineering, materials science, robotics, space, water, biology, and defense.

changesProposed.name: Eclipse Ventures

verified.name: Eclipse Ventures
verified.thesis: Eclipse invests in entrepreneurs building the next generation of companies in the physical economy — the industries that determine resilience, competitiveness, and security.
verified.notes: Source [4] is Wikipedia article about astronomical eclipses, not the venture capital firm. No information about AUM, flagship fund, founded year, headquarters, key partners, or sector focus could be verified from the provided sources. The sources do not list current senior partners by name. Database entry references cannot be used as sources.

changes.current: Eclipse invests in entrepreneurs building the next generation of companies in the physical economy — the industries that determine resilience, competitiveness, and security. The firm partners with innovators transforming the physical economy, bringing operational experience in building factories, shipping hardware, and scaling supply chains.

changesProposed.name: Engine Ventures

verified.name: Engine Ventures
verified.thesis: Engine Ventures invests in early-stage deep tech companies developing hardware-based technologies across fields including biotech, robotics, green energy, agriculture, manufacturing, material science, and quantum computing. In 2023, the investment fund separated from The Engine nonprofit incubator and rebranded as the venture capital firm Engine Ventures.
verified.notes: Engine Ventures is the investment arm that spun out from The Engine nonprofit in 2023. The Engine is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit incubator/accelerator founded by MIT in 2016. Source [4] about SpaceX is unrelated to Engine Ventures and was not used. No current senior partners explicitly named on firm website or Wikipedia. AUM not verifiable from sources. Flagship fund name not verifiable from sources.

changesProposed.name: Point72 Ventures

verified.name: Point72 Ventures
verified.thesis: Thematic-driven investors backing ambitious founders who are building for big outcomes across critical domains. The firm brings deep sector insights, partnership, and hands-on support from seed to pre-IPO to help build enduring companies.
verified.notes: Founded in 2016 (source 0 and 2 both state 'Since 2016'). Steve Cohen is listed as Chairman on the team page (source 1). AUM and flagship fund information not found in provided sources. HQ location not explicitly stated in sources despite database entry claiming Stamford, CT.

changesProposed.name: Thrive Capital Management, LLC

current.name: Thrive Capital Management, LLC
current.hq: New York City, New York, U.S.

verified.name: Thrive Capital Management, LLC
verified.hq: New York City, New York, U.S.
verified.thesis: Thrive Capital is an American venture capital firm that focuses on software and internet investments. The firm invests across early-stage, growth, and late-stage companies while maintaining a strategy of investing heavily in fewer companies to provide closer relationships with founders and greater visibility into portfolio companies.
verified.notes: In August 2024, Thrive raised over $5 billion for Thrive Capital Partners IX, split between a $4 billion late-stage fund and a $1 billion early-stage fund. In 2022, Nitin Nohria, former dean of Harvard Business School, became the firm's first executive chairman.

changes.current: Thrive Capital is a venture capital firm that focuses on software and internet investments. The firm invests across early-stage, growth, and late-stage companies while maintaining a strategy of investing heavily in fewer companies to provide closer relationships with founders. In November 2024, the firm remained faithful to early-stage investing while simultaneously participating in growth and late-stage investments.
changes.verified: Thrive Capital is an American venture capital firm that focuses on software and internet investments. The firm invests across early-stage, growth, and late-stage companies while maintaining a strategy of investing heavily in fewer companies to provide closer relationships with founders and greater visibility into portfolio companies.

changesProposed.notes: In August 2024, Thrive raised over $5 billion for Thrive Capital Partners IX, split between a $4 billion late-stage fund and a $1 billion early-stage fund. In 2022, Nitin Nohria, former dean of Harvard Business School, became the firm's first executive chairman.
